guide_review_response.summary: Iteration 1 of Stage 2 Executor Agent (EA) instance 4 comprehension-gate review. Verdict: APPROVED on iteration 1 -- rare for a HIGH-risk stage, but warranted here. EA-4's pickup verification (V0a-V0g) all PASS and were independently re-verified by Guide instance 5 against current disk state at this review (git log -3, git status --porcelain, git stash list, and pytest tools/ all match EA-4's claims exactly: 11 M / 2 D / 6 ?? entries; 280 passed in 2.36s; stash@{0} byte-identical timestamp 20260424_013002; HEAD chain 9d17e7f -> ccdd82b -> 3b9b1b8). All 27 binding acknowledgments restated verbatim with correct math validation (9 critical_carryovers + 3 g3 + 4 i1 + 7 g4 + 4 g5 = 27); the ack_count_validation block matches Guide-#5 close-out exactly. The 7 remaining Item-2.4 files are enumerated in v3 handoff execution order with concrete per-file approaches: file 7 (self_check.py library variant with project_context kwarg + line 76 disposition); file 8 (blarai_next_task_resolver.py line 92 hardcoded default replacement); file 9 (cleaner.py with explicit g5-3 two-option disposition surfaced at 2.4 closure); files 10-11 (verify-only or trivial); file 12 (legacy _vikunja_client.py defers to 2.6 per g5-4 with explicit escalation contingency if 2.6 leaves line 28 open); file 13 (vikunja_mcp/server.py with the 3-step approach -- startup ctx, chokepoint routing, §D.1 enforcement on list_tasks/search_tasks -- and v4 handoff trigger pre-allocated). Items 2.4.v2 through 2.10 all correctly enumerated with exit semantics. Risk labels are descriptive with reference anchors (no bare numeric labels per d6); 14 risks surfaced including 4 NEW labels addressing g5-1, g5-3, D3, and MCP context-exhaustion. Rollback explicit per Guide-#7 verbatim. Stop discipline correct (no work, no branches, no mutations, no state.resume_fleet() until authorization). Pre-flight verified fleet remains paused with original reason and last_updated_by; EA-4 correctly did NOT re-pause (already paused, persistence-correct behavior). All 8 axes from GUIDE_PROMPT.xml v2.1 ea_comprehension_gate_review pass: objective_match, work_item_coverage, file_scope, verification_strategy, risk_awareness, rollback_awareness, pre_flight_discipline, hard_rules. Zero substantive corrections required. EA-4 may proceed to file 7 (self_check.py) work immediately; three small standing reminders below are not required acknowledgments and require no further re-emission.

independent_disk_verification_of_ea4_cla.intro: Per LA's "mature not minimal" mandate (p3 + p16) and dn14 (do NOT trust EA reports without disk verification), Guide instance 5 independently re-ran the V0 pickup commands at review time (~15 minutes after EA-4 emitted) to confirm no drift between EA-4's observed state and live disk state.
independent_disk_verification_of_ea4_cla.check: Guide-5 observed: 9d17e7f, ccdd82b, 3b9b1b8 (top three). EA-4 V0b observed: 9d17e7f, ccdd82b, 3b9b1b8. EXACT MATCH.

Guide-5 observed: 11 M (10 inherited + GUIDE_PROMPT.xml LA-housekeeping) + 2 D (LA "Temp for responses" rename housekeeping) + 6 ?? (4 inherited tools artifacts + 2 EA-3 reports + 1 new temp_for_responses/) = 19 entries total. EA-4 V0c observed: identical 19 entries with same classification. EXACT MATCH. EA-4 correctly classified all entries per g5-1.

Guide-5 observed: stash@{0}: On main: wake_launcher auto-stash sdo 20260424_013002. EA-4 V0g observed: identical timestamp 20260424_013002. EXACT MATCH. Stash byte-identical to EA-3 close-out and v2/v3 ground-truth.

Guide-5 observed: 280 passed in 2.36s. EA-4 V0e observed: 280 passed in 2.23s. EXACT MATCH on count (280); slight variance in elapsed time is run-to-run jitter, not a regression. Zero test-count drift across EA-3 close, Guide-5 verification, and EA-4 iteration-1.

axis_assessment.axis: EA-4 part_a_objective restates Stage 2 objective + scope correctly, scoped to the resume context (7 of 13 remaining Python entrypoints + items 2.4.v2 through 2.10). Defaults preservation, branch discipline, fleet pause, stash invariance, and v4 handoff fallback all explicitly captured.

All Stage 2 work items enumerated: 2.4 (7 remaining files), 2.4.v2, 2.5 (canonical 7 PS1), 2.5.v2, 2.6 (chokepoint + g5-3 + g5-4 + D3 closure), 2.7, 2.7.v2 (V1-V7 + V8), 2.8 ($BLARAI_PID gate), 2.9 (single commit + pyproject.toml), 2.10 (append-only STATUS). No items dropped or merged.

All 7 file paths correct (cross-checked against v3 handoff and ack g5-2). file 12 is `tools/fleet_observability/_vikunja_client.py` (correct -- the legacy file, NOT the new chokepoint). file 10 is `tools/fleet_ops/diff_builder.py` (correct, contradicting an old stale doc reference per S-1). 19 MCP handlers for file 13 enumerated verbatim and match CLAUDE.md §Vikunja Conventions canonical list.

V1-V7 + V8 strategy explicit in part_d_verification_strategy. V matrix breakdown lists inherited (V1=9 tests, V2=6 tests, V3=6 tests) and NEW (V4=parametrized per refactored entrypoint, V5=PS1 -BlarAIRoot, V6=MCP project_id rejection mock-based, V7=cross-project byte-identity diff). pytest baseline gate ≥280 carried forward.

14 risks surfaced with descriptive labels and reference anchors. Compound labels with anchor disambiguation acceptable per d6. Critical risks all named: argparse-conflict, load_config-defaults-preservation, mcp-server-bypass (C4), mcp-server-context-exhaustion (i1-4 + v3 warning), legacy-name-collision (v2-2 + g5-4), cleaner-fallback-disposition (g5-3), dashboard-maintainer-private-api-2.6 (D3), grep-pattern-coverage (C6), overlay-extension (v2-5), warning-emission (v1-1), w3-blarai-pid-preflight (g4-5), fleet-pause-stash-discipline, single-commit-context-exhaustion, LA-housekeeping-not-EA- violation (g5-1). All g5-N concerns explicitly addressed.

part_f_rollback_acknowledgment block restates Guide-#7 verbatim: "git reset --hard HEAD~1 on chore/platform-extraction (level-1 per RECOVERY.md; branch is local, not pushed). Fleet stays disabled -- no runtime impact from rollback." Per Stage 2 XML <rollback> mandate.

v0_pre_flight verified fleet_paused=true via state.json read. EA-4 correctly identified that the fleet was already paused at session start (by EA-2 during Stage 2 entry) and that no NEW pause command was needed -- pause persists across EA sessions. EA-4 explicitly committed to NOT calling state.resume_fleet() this session (binding acks n=2, n=4, i1-2). Function-name reminder (resume_fleet vs unpause_fleet AttributeError) restated in fleet-pause-stash-discipline risk label.

Master Plan §D.1 (Vikunja project_id HARD RULE) explicitly called out at file 13 plan step (c): `list_tasks(project_id: int | None = None)` MUST become `list_tasks(*, project_id: int)`. Same for search_tasks. Single-commit at 2.9 with pyproject.toml MUST inclusion. No-merge-to-main. stash@{0} untouched. No edits to governance docs (CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, copilot-instructions.md, A1-A10). All 23 binding acks + 4 g5-N restated verbatim.

standing_reminders_for_ea4_during_execut.intro: The three reminders below are NOT required corrections and require no re-emission. They are clarifying notes addressed to EA-4 during execution of the approved work plan.

reminder.topic: library variant vs main()-bearing canonical pattern

standing_reminders_for_ea4_during_execut.reminder: The "canonical pattern" EA-3 proved across 6 files is for main()-bearing entrypoints (argparse + _build_parser + main(argv) + ctx resolve + load_config with explicit paths). For files 7-12 (library_no_main type per v3 handoff), the variant is different: kwarg injection of ctx with backwards-compat None default and auto-resolve fallback. EA-4's file 7 plan correctly identifies this with the "library-style refactor (no argparse)" framing. When you read the 6 EA-3 files end-to-end (per "mature not minimal" mandate committed in next_step_after_authorization), note that build_digest / build_summary helpers in daily_digest.py / weekly_summary.py implement THIS variant (`*, ctx: ProjectContext | None = None` with auto-resolve fallback) -- they are the closest in-tree precedent for the library-variant pattern you will apply to files 7-12.

Changing `list_tasks(project_id: int | None = None)` to `list_tasks(*, project_id: int)` is a signature breaking change. tools/vikunja_mcp/tests/test_server_f5.py (4 tests in current 280 baseline) will likely need test-fixture updates to pass after the file 13 refactor. This is implicit in the V8 defaults-preservation gate + pytest ≥280 baseline, but worth keeping front-of-mind: the test changes are NOT a regression -- they are the correct correlate of the §D.1 enforcement change. Update tests in the same change-set as the handler signature update, not as a separate uncommitted afterthought.

Per ack g5-3 verbatim, cleaner.py:180 fallback "should be addressed in 2.4 scope" -- i.e. as part of file 9 work, not deferred to 2.6 closure. Your part_b_work_plan mentions cleaner.py:180 at BOTH file 9 (Item 2.4) AND Item 2.6 ("MUST close ack g5-3 ... here"). Read these as: file 9 is where the disposition CHOICE and IMPLEMENTATION happen; Item 2.6 is where you VERIFY the disposition closed correctly during the chokepoint audit. Same dual-mention pattern is acceptable for g5-4 / file 12 because g5-4 explicitly defers the implementation to 2.6 (legacy file folding is a 2.6 operation, not 2.4). The two cases are NOT symmetric -- g5-3 closes at 2.4, g5-4 closes at 2.6.

decisions_carried_forward_from_guide_5_v.intro: All four decisions D1-D4 from EA-3's completion report are RATIFIED per Guide-#5 close-out. EA-4 inherits them as established procedure for files 7-13:
decisions_carried_forward_from_guide_5_v.d: REPO_ROOT module-level constant + repo_root kwarg defaulting to REPO_ROOT pattern for any helper with import-side callers (precedent: daily_digest._git_log_oneline_since, weekly_summary._git_log_stats).

Helper ctx-kwarg vs repo_root-kwarg discipline: helpers calling load_config receive `*, ctx: ProjectContext | None = None`; helpers using only REPO_ROOT receive `repo_root: Path = REPO_ROOT`. Two distinct concerns, two distinct kwargs.
dashboard_maintainer.py:113-117 live._request("POST", ...) private-method abuse left for Item 2.6 disposition. Add to your 2.6 disposition list.

Mid-stage handoff timing: emit v4+ handoff before context exhaustion forces emergency-stop mid-MCP-refactor. Per i1-4 + dn15: docs-only commit, no partial-commit refactor, no emergency single-commit attempts. Your "mcp-server-context-exhaustion-watch" risk label correctly pre-allocates this.

guide_review_response.proceed_authorization: PROCEED -- you may begin Item 2.4 file 7 (tools/autonomy_budget/self_check.py) work immediately. Per your committed next_step_after_authorization: read all 6 EA-3-refactored files end-to-end first to internalize both the main()-bearing canonical pattern AND the library variant precedent in build_digest/build_summary (per standing reminder r1), then apply the library variant to file 7. After file 7 is pattern-clean, continue through files 8-13 in v3 handoff execution order. Standing reminders applicable throughout: single Stage-2 commit at item 2.9 (pyproject.toml MUST be in commit); fleet stays paused (NEVER state.resume_fleet -- AttributeError); no merge to main; stash@{0} untouched; append-only STATUS.md at item 2.10. If context exhaustion threatens before item 2.9 (file 13 MCP server is the most likely trigger), emit v4 mid-stage handoff XML following v1/v2/v3 schema (docs-only commit), then stop. On Item 2.4 closure (all 13 files pattern-clean + cleaner.py:180 dispositioned per g5-3), proceed to 2.4.v2 verification, then items 2.5 through 2.10. On Item 2.6 closure: ensure legacy fleet_observability/_vikunja_client.py:28 load_config callsite is CLOSED (per g5-4); if 2.6 disposition leaves it open, escalate to Guide before 2.9 commit. On Item 2.10 closure: emit a closing-handoff-to-LA per the closing_handoff_to_la_at_stage_2_close block in the EA-4 initiation pointer.
